WSAResetEvent

Resets the state of the specified event object to nonsignaled.

BOOL WSAResetEvent(
  WSAEVENT hEvent  
);

Parameters

hEvent
[in] Handle that identifies an open event object handle.

Return Values

If the WSAResetEvent function succeeds, the return value is TRUE. If the function fails, the return value is FALSE. To get extended error information, call WSAGetLastError.

Remarks

The WSAResetEvent function is used to set the state of the event object to nonsignaled.

WSAResetEvent is equivalent to ResetEvent.

Requirements

Header: Declared in winsockx.h, for include use Xtl.h.
Import Library: Use Xnet.lib for titles that only support system link play, XOnline.lib for titles that support online play. Use the secure versions of the library file (Xnets.lib or XOnlines.lib) for final testing.

See Also

Winsock Overview, Winsock Functions | WSACloseEvent, WSACreateEvent, WSASetEvent
